articles.title: Combining Senolytics and Stem Cells Shows Promise in Mice
articles.summary: A new study associated with Immorta Bio suggests that combining a senolytic vaccine with mesenchymal stem cells might create a synergistic impact. However, the findings rest on acute, artificially induced injury models rather than natural aging [1]. […]

articles.title: US longevity policy takes center stage in Washington
articles.summary: A4LI’s Brenda Eap highlights a turning point for healthspan advocacy as CMS and FDA engagement signals a Washington shift. Washington DC has always been a city of change – a place where rules are rewritten and intellectuals debate the future of the nation. But from June 29 to July 1, 2026, the energy felt entirely […] The post US longevity policy takes center stage in Washington appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Longevity Ready Maryland Act: A model for aging policy?
articles.summary: Maryland is asking whether governments should prepare for longer lives the same way they plan for roads, schools and the economy. What happens when a government finally admits that people living longer isn’t a future problem – it’s today’s reality? Most US states have published reports, convened commissions or launched task forces to prepare for […] The post Longevity Ready Maryland Act: A model for aging policy? appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Lys Therapeutics secures $29m for brain therapy
articles.summary: French biotech pushes a single brain-protecting therapy toward the clinic, raising fresh questions about the future of neurological medicine. What if one drug could help someone rushed into the emergency room after a stroke and also slow the steady progression of Parkinson’s disease? At first glance, the idea sounds almost contradictory. Stroke is sudden, measured […] The post Lys Therapeutics secures $29m for brain therapy appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Kenai Therapeutics completes enrollment for Parkinson’s cell therapy trial
articles.summary: Kenai Therapeutics has completed enrollment in the REPLACE Phase 1b/2a clinical trial of RNDP-001 for moderate to moderate-severe idiopathic Parkinson’s disease, the company announced on 7 July 2026, in San Diego. RNDP-001 is an off-the-shelf, allogeneic neuron replacement cell therapy designed to restore dopaminergic neurons lost in Parkinson’s disease. The open-label, multicenter REPLACE study is […] The post Kenai Therapeutics completes enrollment for Parkinson’s cell therapy trial appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: FibroBiologics produces third batch of experimental cell therapy for diabetic foot ulcers
articles.summary: FibroBiologics completed manufacturing of a third batch of its CYWC628 drug product for the ongoing Phase 1/2 clinical trial in patients with diabetic foot ulcers. The batch was produced under cGMP standards and is undergoing required safety and quality testing ahead of release for clinical use. Upon release of the third batch, the company expects […] The post FibroBiologics produces third batch of experimental cell therapy for diabetic foot ulcers appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Elysium pilot study links Basis to reduced menopause symptoms
articles.summary: Elysium reported results from an open-label pilot study of 40 healthy women over 35, 32 of whom self-reported symptoms associated with the menopause transition and eight who reported minimal or no symptoms. Participants received Basis for seven days. After seven days, participants in the symptom group self-reported reductions of 50 percent or more in the […] The post Elysium pilot study links Basis to reduced menopause symptoms appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Insilico Medicine expects revenue to nearly quadruple to over $100m
articles.summary: Insilico Medicine, a generative AI-driven clinical-stage biotech listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (03696.HK), issued a positive profit alert for the six months ended 30 June 2026. The company expects revenue of approximately USD102.5 million to USD106.5 million, a year-on-year increase of about 272.7% to 287.3%. Net profit is projected at approximately USD33.5 million […] The post Insilico Medicine expects revenue to nearly quadruple to over $100m appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Amprion secures multi-million-dollar funding to expand brain disease diagnostic testing
articles.summary: Amprion, headquartered in San Diego, announced on 8 July 2026 that it reached an agreement with Decathlon Capital Partners for a multi-million-dollar growth-funding investment to expand diagnostic testing capacity for neurodegenerative diseases, including Parkinson’s disease, Lewy body dementia and Alzheimer’s disease. According to the company, the tailored financing provides flexible amortization, is non-dilutive to existing […] The post Amprion secures multi-million-dollar funding to expand brain disease diagnostic testing appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: An Experimental Proposal for Blocking Ambient Radiation
articles.summary: A perspective published in Aging and Disease has recommended the use of underground laboratory space in order to remove the effects of surface radiation on biological clocks. A question of entropy The cascading failure of bodily […]

articles.title: Scientists decipher how a psychedelic substance is created, then engineer a plant to produce several at once
articles.summary: Long before scientists began studying them in the lab, mind-altering substances were already being gathered from plants, fungi and even animals for use in rituals, healing practices and mental health treatment. Researchers at the Weizmann Institute of Science have now managed to bring together in a single organism five psychedelic substances that in nature are scattered across the tree of life.

articles.title: Creating synthetic life in a lab? SpudCell falls short of the goal, but raises even more useful questions
articles.summary: Nature is beautiful, powerful and essential. But nature is not always gentle. The same biological world that gives rise to forests, coral reefs and human life also produces infections, cancer, genetic disease, crop blights and toxins. Natural processes can heal, sustain and inspire, but they can also destroy.

articles.title: Drawing the line: Virtual fences trigger the same cattle behavior as physical ones
articles.summary: Virtual fences could make managing grazing livestock on farms more flexible and more efficient while improving animal welfare. A new study by the University of Göttingen shows that virtual fences trigger behavior in cattle similar to that caused by conventional electric fences, in terms of how they move around the field. This finding puts commonly expressed concerns about animal welfare into perspective. The results were published in the journal Animal.

articles.title: New platform creates digital map for marine biobanks
articles.summary: A new digital platform developed under the leadership of CIIMAR is making Portugal's marine biodiversity more accessible by bringing together thousands of biological resources into a single access point. The Blue Biobanks Digital Research Platform aims to bridge scientific research and industry by simplifying the identification of organisms and biological materials with potential applications in areas such as biotechnology, bioremediation, aquaculture, health, cosmetics and many others.

articles.title: Bacteria turn dissolved uranium into stable compound in 130 days, study finds
articles.summary: Researchers at the Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR), together with Wismut GmbH and scientists from the University of Granada in Spain, have demonstrated for the first time that bacteria can convert uranium dissolved in water into a stable chemical compound when they have access to glycerol as a food source.

articles.title: Assessing lab animals with AI
articles.summary: Rutgers Office for Research (OfR) leaders collaborated with researchers around the world to develop an artificial intelligence (AI) program that has the potential to revolutionize lab research.

articles.title: Engineered ADARs enable precision A-to-G base editing of DNA
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 10 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03223-z Bystander edits are minimized by engineering an adenosine deaminase acting on RNA for DNA base editing.

articles.title: Efficient in vivo cytosine base editing using virus-like particles with uracil DNA glycosylase inhibition
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 10 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03227-9 In vivo cytosine base editing is made efficient with potent glycosylase inhibition.

articles.title: Wearables to track plant health: Farmers could use real-time information to manage crop conditions
articles.summary: A smartwatch can tell us the level of oxygen in our blood, when our sleep is restless or the number of steps we take in a day. Now imagine that kind of tracking ability for plants. By the time farmers see curling leaves or stunted growth in their fields, their crops may already have spent days under stress.

articles.title: Biotech’s digital twin dilemma
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03211-3 From virtual patients to simulated cells and organs-on-chips, digital twins are forecasting disease and guiding drug development. Scientists now must figure out how to define, validate and deploy these models.

articles.title: Stablecoin market cap has shrunk by $10 billion since May, but analyst sees no reason to panic
articles.summary: The market shrank by $7.7 billion in June alone, the largest dollar amount since May 2022's Terra-Luna crash, but stablecoins will likely resume their long-term growth, one analyst said.

articles.title: Pakistan crypto chief seeks dialogue after scholar rules against crypto payments
articles.summary: Pakistan’s virtual-assets regulator called for continued dialogue on the treatment of digital assets after meeting an Islamic scholar who backed a ruling against purchases made with crypto.

articles.title: Bitcoin is nearing a power law support line Fidelity has tracked since 2015
articles.summary: The group's Dir. of Global Macro Jurien Timmer calls it an accumulation zone but notes the lack of a catalyst to bounce yet.

articles.title: Cambridge study puts Ethereum near the lower end of PoS energy intensity
articles.summary: Cambridge estimated that Ethereum consumes 7.87 GWh annually and has the second-lowest market-value-adjusted energy intensity among the proof-of-stake networks studied.

articles.title: Bitcoin, ether little changed as U.S. launches fresh Iran strikes
articles.summary: The U.S. hit Iran for the third time this week and Tehran has reportedly closed the Strait of Hormuz again.

articles.title: Ripple once weighed shutting down and handing XRP to shareholders, CEO says
articles.summary: Ripple's Brad Garlinghouse says he and co-founder Chris Larsen considered winding the company down and handing its XRP to shareholders before deciding to fight the 2020 lawsuit.

articles.title: Bitcoin’s BIP 110 fork deadline nears with miner support at zero
articles.summary: The BIP 110 proposal would cap arbitrary data on Bitcoin for a year, but Saylor, Adam Back and others say turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create a bigger risk than the spam itself.

articles.title: Bitcoin bulls Michael Saylor, Adam Back slam BIP-110 Ordinals proposal
articles.summary: The ongoing debate over a proposed temporary fork comes despite a broad downturn in Ordinals transaction activity over the last two years.
